title: Quiz 4 — Programs, Efficiency, and the Field (Lessons 7–9)

questions.prompt: A module is imported twice in the same process. Its top-level code:
choices: runs twice
runs once; the second import is a sys.modules cache hit
runs zero times — only definitions are read
raises ImportError

questions.prompt: In a Python traceback, the line that actually raised is found:
choices: on the first line, right after the header
wherever the deepest indentation is
in the last frame shown — read tracebacks bottom-up
in the middle frame

questions.prompt: The point of `if __name__ == "__main__":` is that the file:
choices: can be imported without side effects, running main() only as a script
loads measurably faster
hides its private functions from importers
declares its entry point to the interpreter
changing the most suspicious line and rerunning
a minimal reproduction and a falsifiable hypothesis
wrapping the failure in try/except to gather data
stepping through every line in the debugger
n, log n, n log n, n²
log n, n, n log n, n²
log n, n log n, n, n²
n, n log n, n², log n

questions.prompt: `x in big_set` beats `x in big_list` because:
choices: sets keep their elements sorted for bisection
lists store boxed values; sets store raw ones
hashing gives sets roughly constant-time membership; lists scan linearly
sets memoize previous membership queries

questions.prompt: Which course in this degree proves that some problems cannot be computed at all?
choices: CS 301 — Algorithms
CS 350 — Software Engineering
CS 330 — Database Systems
CS 370 — Theory of Computation
